narrative: Dear [Student's Name], imagine the admissions team looking at your application not just as a stack of numbers, but as the opening chapters of your story as a future lawyer—and right now, those chapters have painted a picture of real challenges ahead on the path to passing the bar exam. Your Year 3 decile of 3 stood out as the biggest signal, whispering that even after time to settle in, your grades placed you in the lower third of your peers, a pattern that echoed strongly from your Year 1 decile of 3, suggesting consistency in a tough academic climb. Layered on top was your LSAT score of 34, which, while solid for many, tipped the scales here toward struggle in the high-stakes bar world we know so well. Your middle family income added another thread, as it often means fewer resources for the intensive prep that top performers lean on. Then there's your undergraduate GPA of 2.8, reinforcing that academic hurdles have been a recurring theme from the start. As a white male going full-time, those details fit a profile our experience shows can face steeper odds without standout strengths to counterbalance. All together, these painted a vivid image of a 57% chance of not crossing the bar finish line, leading us to pause on your application with a heavy heart—it's not about your potential or drive, but about stewarding spots for those whose stories align most closely with bar success. You've got grit in spades; consider bolstering those early grades or LSAT edges elsewhere. We're rooting for your next chapter.
